entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: This course gives students a glimpse into how the ideas that guide our study of arithmetic were first discovered.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: This course is intended to follow Part 1; however, the order may be reversed to facilitate combining students.

rows.label: Story of Arithmetic: Part 2
rows.scheduleInfo:
Story of Arithmetic: Part 2
1 time/week 15 min
rows.books: Number Stories of Long Ago||Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.||Student World Atlas 6th Ed.

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Story of Arithmetic: Part 2
☐ Term 1: Familiarize yourself with the "lattice method." (See Lesson 6.)

books.title: Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.

blocks.text: Optional: Use a picture, map, or engaging question to prepare the student to receive the idea from the day’s lesson. Talk about one or two unknown words that will be crucial to understanding.
Read the new passage out loud or have the student read it orally or silently.
Have the student narrate orally or in writing.
• Give students an opportunity to share their thoughts on the lesson and any connections made.
Optional: Attempt to recreate some of the problems from the stories.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Multiplication: Introduction
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ INTRO
We left off when people had learned to add and subtract pretty easily, but multiplication and division were a problem. This year, we will see how these methods were developed. We begin in the 1100s. "Moor" is an old word for a Muslim person from regions in southern Spain and northern Africa. We don't use it anymore.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.65-66 "So you wonder" - "Leonardo and Johann."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 2

⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.66-67 "When Leonardo of" - "we are taught."

➜ PRACTICE
Try multiplying 36 x 5 Leonardo's way.

⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.67-71 "It was about" - "Leonardo and Johann."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 4
lessons.body:
Multiplication: The Grating Plan 
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.71-72 "I don't see" - "answered the Story-Teller."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 5
lessons.body:
Practice: Cuthbert's Method
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ STUDY
Study the pictures. The problem is 934 x 314. Work it out our way, and then look again at the pictures. Can you figure out how this method works?
⍞ Image Resource: Number Stories of Long Ago "From the First Printed Arithmetic" p.70

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 6
lessons.body:
Practice: The Lattice Method
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ VIEW
∞ Video Link: Lattice Multiplication (6:12)

➜ PRACTICE
Look again at the pictures. Use the lattice method to multiply 934 x 314. Which of the four methods shown matches your work? Make up another problem and use the lattice method to solve it. Check your work using our method.
⍞ Image Resource: Number Stories of Long Ago "From the First Printed Arithmetic" p.70

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
Division: Scratch and Galley Methods
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.75-77 "Since this is" - "learned in school."

➜ STUDY
Look at the image. Can you find 1728 and 12? How many times will 12 go into 17? Work the problem our way. Can you find where Filippo recorded his answer?
⍞ Image Resource: Number Stories of Long Ago "How Filippo Proved His Work" p.75
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
1st Arithmetic Bk, England (1522)
1st Arithmetic Bk, Mexico (1556)

⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.77-80 "After Filippo had" - "still more simple."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 9
lessons.body:
Division: Adriaen and Michael 
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.80 "When Filippo became" - "two centuries later."

➜ STUDY
"How Adriaen Divided." Can you find the 150 and the 6? How many times will 6 go into 15? [2 with 3 left over] Where did Adriaen record the 2 and the 3? Can you figure out the rest?

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 10
lessons.body:
Division: Conclusion
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.81-82 "If you had" - "laughed the Crowd."

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 11
lessons.body:
Practice

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ PRACTICE
How many 5's will go into 565? Solve using manipulatives first and then with long division.

➜ VIEW
This teacher is showing us three different algorithms for solving the same problem. An algorithm is a sort of shortcut for getting an answer without using real objects. Which do you think is easiest? Why?
∞ Video Link: Three Methods of Division (5:10)

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 14
lessons.body:
Fractions: Ahmes of Egypt
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago, Historical Atlas of the World

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.86-87 "When Ahmes had" - "the Christian era."

➜ LOCATE 
⍞ Map Resource: Historical Atlas of the World "International Trade 1350-1450 A.D." p.28-29
Find Alexandria. What can you tell about this city by looking at the map?

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 15
lessons.body:
Fractions: Heron and Jakob
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ INTRO
Look at an analog clock. Can you discover how it shows fractions? What about a digital clock?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.87-88 "When Heron went" - "else thought so."

➜ DISCUSS
Have you ever heard someone complain about "new-fangled math" like Jakob's teacher? How might you answer that person, now that you have studied some of the history of math?

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 16
lessons.body:
Fractions: Simon of Belgium
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago, Student World Atlas

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.89-91 "It was nearly" - "started for bed."

➜ LOCATE 
⍞ Map Resource: Student World Atlas "Political Map of Europe" p.83
Belgium

➜ DISCUSS
What did you think when you read about "fashions" in arithmetic? Do you think other subjects have fashions, too?

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 18
lessons.body:
Number Puzzles: Ching and Menes
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.95-98 "In my land" - "secrets of arithmetic."

➜ PRACTICE
Test Menes's theory that if you take the difference of a number and its reverse, this difference is always divisible by 9.

lessons.title: 15m Story of Arithmetic: Part 2 - Lesson 19
lessons.body:
Number Puzzles: Heron and Gerbert
⍞ Materials: Number Stories of Long Ago

➜ RECAP
What did you read last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.98-99 "One of the" - "do the trick?"

➜ STUDY
The box of numbers. Can you figure out how Adriaen chose his numbers so that the sum would be 29,997? [Try adding 2376 + 7623 and 4152 + 5847. Can you figure out the rest?]
⍞ Image Resource: Number Stories of Long Ago, p.98-99
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
Heron of Alexandria (10 A.D.-70 A.D.)

terms.content: · Number Stories of Long Ago: Tell what you know about how methods of multiplication have changed over time.
· Number Stories of Long Ago: Tell what you know about how fractions have changed over time OR Tell about one of the number puzzles you found interesting.
· Number Stories of Long Ago: Tell about a story problem or puzzle you found interesting this term.
